GRN_tool: pySCENIC (GRNBoost2 default; GENIE3 deprecated for scale)
cell_types: PBMCs (10k); Mouse S1 cortex (3k); Human tumor biopsies (melanoma/breast/HNSCC)
validation_approach: cisTarget motif enrichment database (self-validation — circular; not external ChIP-seq)
RNA_only_recommendation: This protocol applies to the reconstruction of GRNs from single-cell transcriptomics experiments. As input the SCENIC pipeline needs a gene expression matrix. No ATAC/chromatin data required.
performance_without_ATAC: NR — RNA-only protocol; ATAC not evaluated
GRNBoost2_vs_GENIE3: GRNBoost2 default — 10x faster than GENIE3 with equivalent network quality
multi_run_stability: WARNING: GRNBoost2 is stochastic (random-forest). Authors note repeated runs yield slightly different weights but do not mandate multi-run aggregation in core protocol.
known_failure_modes: 1) Stochasticity in GRNBoost2 weights across runs; 2) Cannot capture TF activity regulated post-translationally (phosphorylation/ligand); 3) cisTarget workers OOM-kill silently if RAM exhausted — no error raised
notes: CRITICAL SCALING GAP: All compute benchmarks based on 10k cells. DAM-DRUG microglial subset estimated 50k-200k cells. RAM scaling for cisTarget is non-linear — 23 GB at 10k may approach 100-200+ GB at 100k cells. 512 GB HPC node spec in research plan remains appropriate but margin is tighter than it appears. Recommend running on microglial subset only (not full dataset). Minimum cell threshold (20/cluster) is not a concern: Mathys et al. microglia ~1900 cells = 95x above minimum. pySCENIC v0.9.18 (paper) vs v0.12.1 (research plan) — verify changelog for breaking changes.

GRN_tool: SCENIC+ vs. pySCENIC vs. CellOracle vs. Pando vs. FigR vs. GRaNIE vs. ArchR/Signac
cell_types: Human PBMCs; Human ENCODE cell lines (GM12878/IMR90/MCF7/HepG2/PC3/K562/Panc1/HCT116); Human melanoma; Drosophila retina; Human+Mouse motor cortex (incl. MICROGLIA — high DAM-DRUG relevance)
validation_approach: ChIP-seq overlap (309 ENCODE datasets + UniBind); PERTURB-seq/CRISPRi (157 TF knockdown datasets ENCODE); STARR-seq reporter assays; Hi-C (5 cell lines) — multi-assay gold standard
RAM_GRNBoost2_GB: 21-461 (scales with N cells; 25k multiome cells = 461 GB)
RAM_cisTarget_GB: 21-461 (integrated — SCENIC+ does not separate steps cleanly)
ATAC_required: Y (scATAC-seq required; pseudo-multiome fallback available if independent bulk/scATAC exists)
RNA_only_recommendation: RNA-only pySCENIC remains highly competitive baseline for TF-to-gene recovery when ATAC unavailable. ATAC-only (ArchR/Signac) yields HIGH FALSE-POSITIVE TF predictions — worse than RNA-only pySCENIC.
performance_without_ATAC: DELTA AUROC = +0.04 (SCENIC+ 0.42 vs pySCENIC 0.38) — MARGINAL. Decision threshold 0.10 NOT crossed.
min_cells_per_cluster: NR (SCENIC+ designed for multiome; low-cell low-coverage failure explicitly noted)
GRNBoost2_vs_GENIE3: SCENIC+ strictly requires ATAC; pySCENIC uses GRNBoost2 (RNA-only)
multi_run_stability: Multi-run not addressed; stochasticity inherited from GRNBoost2 component
known_failure_modes: 1) Requires paired multiome (or pseudo-multiome with independent ATAC); 2) Fails at <80 cells or <3000 ATAC fragments; 3) Biased toward activation — repressive interactions poorly recovered; 4) Enhancer-gene distance approximation limits physical loop resolution; 5) RAM 461 GB at 25k cells — scales prohibitively for 100k+ cells
notes: DECISION: ΔAUROC +0.04 < threshold 0.10 → RNA-only pySCENIC sufficient; SCENIC+ not required. CRITICAL BONUS: DAM-DRUG priority TFs ALL validated — IRF8 (dendritic/PBMC) + SPI1/PU.1 (monocytes/PBMC) + CEBPB (HepG2) + MITF (melanoma) + RUNX1 (cortex/NK). Strongest available published evidence that pySCENIC can recover these exact TFs. ABSOLUTE ACCURACY CAVEAT: Even SCENIC+ best-case AUROC=0.42 means ~58% of predicted TF-target links are false positives by ChIP-seq ground truth — both tools operate at low absolute precision. Must be disclosed as manuscript limitation. PICO 2b FULLY CLOSED.
